title: Performium Reborn
description: Lightweight vanilla+ optimization modpack, UI and QoL stuff, fork of Performium
body:
# About
**Performium Reborn** is a fork of [**Performium**](https://modrinth.com/modpack/performium-was-taken) modpack. A lightweight (about 50 mods) vanilla+ modpack focused on essential improvements: optimization, borderless fullscreen, quality-of-life tweaks, smooth UI animations, and other must-have features every player installs first

My project is different (hopefully for the better) from the original, so if you need a direct sequel without changes, try [**Performium: Continued**](https://modrinth.com/modpack/performium-continued)

### Differences from other modpacks
Unlike other modpacks, **Performium Reborn** contains a [**Voxy**](https://modrinth.com/mod/voxy) mod and does not use unnecessary mods (like [**CalcMod**](https://modrinth.com/plugin/calcmod), which is shoved into each optimization modpack) so that the build remains lightweight

### Concept 
Designed as a clean, stable base for both regular gameplay and building your own modpacks. You can create any forks, please mention the original

### Also
Looking for a modpack with [**VulkanMod**](https://modrinth.com/mod/vulkanmod)? Check out [**Vulkanium**](https://modrinth.com/modpack/vulkaniumpack)
### Neoforge
You can find the neoforge version [here](https://modrinth.com/modpack/neoperformium-reborn)

# Usage tips
Highly recomended pregenerate world with chunky before playing:
1. /chunky center
2. /chunky radius
3. /chunky shape circle
4. /chunky start


# Versioning System
`vX.Y`

* **Major version (`X`)** — large/game-changing updates that significantly affect the modpack experience (new core mods, major reworks, etc.)
* **Minor version (`Y`)** — smaller additions, tweaks, fixes

Example: `v1.0`

The modpack also has an internal release state shown on Modrinth:

* **Alpha** — incomplete or unstable, many planned mods/features may be missing
* **Beta** — mostly complete, but some non-critical mods may still be outdated or unavailable
* **Release** — intended feature set is fully implemented and stable

The release state is not included in the modpack name, since it only reflects how complete the current version is relative to its intended mod list
